political.s: CVS Health spent $10.3 million on federal lobbying and donated $5 million to dark-money group opposing Medicare for All and public option proposals. The company's PAC contributed $505,000 to federal candidates in 2023-2024, with a historical lean toward Republican recipients.

charity.s: CVS Health Foundation focuses on health access, chronic disease management, tobacco cessation, and community health. Foundation grants range from $1,000 to $1 million with emphasis on health equity, mental health services, and homeless support programs across Northeast and operationally-based communities.
charity.focus: Health care access, chronic disease management, tobacco prevention, mental health, homelessness

environment.s: CVS Health committed to 50% environmental impact reduction by 2030 and net-zero emissions by 2050. Company has implemented energy efficiency programs, eliminated single-use bags in some locations, and achieved 9% Scope 3 emissions reduction. Science-based emissions targets verified by third-party auditor.

labor.s: CVS faced significant labor disputes with UFCW locals in 2024, including unfair labor practice strikes and allegations of surveillance, worker intimidation, and denial of union access. Workers reported inadequate wages (under $20/hour for store clerks), unaffordable health benefits, and safety concerns. Recent tentative agreement includes wage increases and staffing guarantees.

dei.s: CVS Health maintains comprehensive DEI programs including mandatory leadership training on diversity and inclusion, colleague resource groups, disability inclusion initiatives (100 score on Disability Equality Index), and health equity leadership position focused on hypertension, depression, and maternal health disparities.

privacy.s: CVS Health paid $2.25 million in 2009 HIPAA settlement for improper disposal of protected health information and faced recent probe for alleged use of patient data for political lobbying in Louisiana. Company maintains HIPAA compliance policies but has history of privacy violations and regulatory oversight.

execPay.s: CEO David Joyner earned $21.2 million in total compensation in 2025 including $14.5 million in stock awards. Top executives average $13 million annually. Pay ratio and median employee compensation data not clearly disclosed in available sources.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
